title: Aligning Marketing and Finance for Business Growth
content:
Achieving true alignment within an organization requires a fundamental cultural shift in how marketing and finance approach measurement and decision-making. Marketing teams must provide the same rigorous validation and forecasting that financial executives expect from other major investments. Several leading brands are already successfully working to bridge this gap and create powerful alignment. For example, McDonald’s made CMO-CFO alignment a central pillar of its recent global growth strategy. Their finance and marketing teams established a shared language grounded in hard data, tangible impact, and measurable business outcomes.

When creativity and commercial strategy move in lockstep, it transforms both the brand and the underlying business. AT&T took similar steps by prioritizing alignment between its marketing and finance leadership to drive growth. Successful marketing teams use shared KPIs to clearly demonstrate their financial impact, which naturally makes it easier for CFOs to support new initiatives. Having financial leaders who are fluent in the practice of marketing goes a long way toward building trust. If global giants see immense value in bridging the CMO-CFO divide, newer and smaller brands can certainly benefit from the same strategy.
